Home › Scholarships › Australia, Canada, Turkey
Australia, Canada, Turkey

Turkish Government Scholarships for International Students in Australia and Canada

Published 9 Jun 20266 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 9 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to Turkish Government Scholarships

Turkish Government Scholarships are available for international students in Australia and Canada, providing them with the opportunity to pursue their higher education with Turkish funding. These scholarships are designed to promote cultural exchange and academic excellence, and they are open to students from various countries, including those residing in Australia and Canada. The scholarships are awarded by the Turkish government to support international students in their academic endeavors, covering various fields of study.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for Turkish Government Scholarships, international students in Australia and Canada must belong to specific visa categories, including:

* Subclass 500 (Student) visa for students in Australia

* Study permit for students in Canada

* e-Visa for students traveling to Turkey

* Residence permit for students residing in Turkey

* Other visa categories as specified by the Turkish government

Available Scholarships

The Turkish government offers various scholarships to international students, including:

Turkey Scholarships for International Students

The Turkey Scholarships for International Students program provides funding for international students to pursue their higher education in Turkey. The coverage amount varies depending on the program, but it can include tuition fees, accommodation, and living expenses. Eligibility criteria include being a citizen of a country other than Turkey, meeting the academic requirements of the chosen program, and being under the age of 25 for undergraduate programs. The deadline for application is typically in February or March each year. For more information, visit https://www.turkiyescholarships.org/.

Mevlana Exchange Program

The Mevlana Exchange Program is a scholarship program that allows international students to study in Turkey for a semester or a year. The coverage amount includes a monthly stipend of 750 TL, accommodation, and tuition fees. Eligibility criteria include being a student at a higher education institution, meeting the language requirements of the host institution, and being under the age of 35. The deadline for application is typically in June or July each year. For more information, visit https://www.mevlana.org.tr/.

Research Scholarship Program

The Research Scholarship Program is a scholarship program that provides funding for international researchers to conduct research in Turkey. The coverage amount includes a monthly stipend of 2,500 TL, accommodation, and research expenses. Eligibility criteria include having a master's or PhD degree, meeting the language requirements of the host institution, and being under the age of 40. The deadline for application is typically in February or March each year. For more information, visit https://www.tubitatech.gov.tr/.

Undergraduate Scholarship Program

The Undergraduate Scholarship Program is a scholarship program that provides funding for international students to pursue their undergraduate studies in Turkey. The coverage amount includes tuition fees, accommodation, and living expenses. Eligibility criteria include being a citizen of a country other than Turkey, meeting the academic requirements of the chosen program, and being under the age of 25. The deadline for application is typically in February or March each year. For more information, visit https://www.turkiyescholarships.org/.

Graduate Scholarship Program

The Graduate Scholarship Program is a scholarship program that provides funding for international students to pursue their graduate studies in Turkey. The coverage amount includes tuition fees, accommodation, and living expenses. Eligibility criteria include being a citizen of a country other than Turkey, meeting the academic requirements of the chosen program, and being under the age of 30 for master's programs and 35 for PhD programs. The deadline for application is typically in February or March each year. For more information, visit https://www.turkiyescholarships.org/.

Application Steps

To apply for Turkish Government Scholarships, international students in Australia and Canada must follow these steps:

  • Check the eligibility criteria for the chosen scholarship program.

  • Prepare the required documents, including a valid passport, academic transcripts, and language proficiency certificates.

  • Choose the program and university to apply to.

  • Submit the application through the online application portal.

  • Wait for the application to be processed and reviewed.

  • Take the Turkish language proficiency test, if required.

  • Receive the scholarship award letter, if selected.

  • Apply for a student visa to study in Turkey, if required.

Documents Checklist

To apply for Turkish Government Scholarships, international students in Australia and Canada must provide the following documents:

* A valid passport

* Academic transcripts

* Language proficiency certificates (English or Turkish)

* A letter of acceptance from the chosen university

* A resume or CV

* A personal statement or motivation letter

* Proof of financial support

* Health insurance

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is the deadline for applying for Turkish Government Scholarships?

The deadline for applying for Turkish Government Scholarships varies depending on the program, but it is typically in February or March each year.

  • Can I apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if I am already studying in Turkey?

Yes, you can apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if you are already studying in Turkey, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and follow the application procedures.

  • Do I need to speak Turkish to apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship?

No, you do not need to speak Turkish to apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship, but you may need to take a language proficiency test as part of the application process.

  • Can I apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if I am not a citizen of Australia or Canada?

Yes, you can apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if you are not a citizen of Australia or Canada, but you must meet the eligibility criteria and follow the application procedures.

  • How much funding is provided by Turkish Government Scholarships?

The amount of funding provided by Turkish Government Scholarships varies depending on the program, but it can include tuition fees, accommodation, and living expenses.

  • Can I apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if I am already receiving a scholarship from another organization?

No, you cannot apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship if you are already receiving a scholarship from another organization, as this may be considered a conflict of interest.

  • How do I apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship?

To apply for a Turkish Government Scholarship, you must submit an application through the online application portal and provide the required documents.

  • What are the benefits of receiving a Turkish Government Scholarship?

The benefits of receiving a Turkish Government Scholarship include funding for tuition fees, accommodation, and living expenses, as well as the opportunity to study in Turkey and experience Turkish culture.

External Resources

For more information on Turkish Government Scholarships, visit the official website of the Turkish Ministry of Education or the Turkish Embassy or Consulate in your home country.

Conclusion

Turkish Government Scholarhips provide international students in Australia and Canada with the opportunity to pursue their higher education in Turkey with funding and support. To learn more about scholarships and education opportunities, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog
